more.title: FUSA Wins Outstanding Payments Case Against PSL Club
more.description: FUSA has confirmed that former Kruger United players have received outstanding payments following a successful PSL Dispute Resolution Chamber ruling, with more cases still pending.

more.title: Sports betting can become a trap, warns personal finance expert
more.description: Major sporting events are driving a surge in sports betting, but personal finance expert Laura du Preez warns that chasing quick wins can lead to debt, financial stress and repeated losses.

more.description: Captain Ronwen Williams says next year's AFCON is their next mission.

more.title: SANDF Deployed to Clermont as Wave of Lootings Rocks Durban Amid Immigration Protests
more.description: CLERMONT, KwaZulu-Natal — The South African National Defence Force has been deployed to KwaDabeka in Clermont, west of Durban, following a devastating wave of business lootings that struck overnight amid ongoing nationwide protests targeting undocumented immigrants. The unrest has left local business owners reeling after two supermarkets and a dental practice within a shopping centre [...]

more.title: The Springboks owe Ellis Park a statement performance against England
more.description: The world champions have named arguably their strongest possible matchday 23 for Saturday’s Test against England, leaving little excuse for anything other than a convincing victory at Ellis Park.

more.title: Migration committee to visit Beitbridge border to assess repatriation process
more.description: About 10,000 Malawian nationals have been camped in Musina for the past three days while awaiting documentation from the department to enable them to cross the border.

more.description: Industry-funded initiative undertaken in collaboration with the RTMC, National Consumer Commission and Retail Motor Industry Organisation.

more.title: South Africa introduces mandatory online travel declarations for millions of international travellers
more.description: South Africa has made online traveller declarations mandatory for everyone entering or leaving the country, marking a major step in the government's efforts to digitise customs procedures and strengthen border management.

more.title: South Africa’s 30 June protests leave hundreds arrested as tensions spread nationwide
more.description: A wave of anti-migrant protests swept across South Africa this week amid the 30 June deadline, leading to more than 900 arrests nationwide as tensions over illegal immigration once again spilled onto the streets, Cape {town} Etc reports. While most marches remained peaceful, authorities confirmed that several areas experienced outbreaks of looting, public violence and attacks on foreign-owned businesses, raising fears... more.title: South African Legal AI Startup Anvaya Wins Local Start-up World Cup, Advances to Silicon Valley Final
more.description: South African legal artificial intelligence startup Anvaya has clinched the top prize at the South African chapter of the Start-up World Cup, earning a place at the competition’s global final in Silicon Valley. more.title: Ramaphosa Urges Africa to Accelerate AI Adoption at First Google Cloud Summit on the Continent
more.description: President Cyril Ramaphosa has urged South Africa and the wider African continent to accelerate the adoption of artificial intelligence and cloud technologies, warning that hesitation will leave nations trailing in the global digital race. Delivering the keynote at the first Google Cloud Summit on African soil, held at the Sandton Convention Centre on Wednesday, Ramaphosa [...]

more.title: Anti-illegal immigration activists in KuGompo urge govt to address their grievances
more.description: In the Eastern Cape, police arrested 208 undocumented foreign nationals, while 21 people were arrested for offences linked to the protests.

more.title: Chidimma Adetshina deportation case deepens after Cape Town arrest
more.description: Chidimma Adetshina’s deportation case is set to return to the Cape Town Regional Court on 16 July after the former Miss South Africa finalist and reigning Miss Universe Nigeria was arrested in Summer Greens earlier this month, reports Cape {town} Etc. The Department of Home Affairs alleges that Adetshina and her minor son were living in South Africa without lawful...
